valueString.value: This extension is based on a fragment of the code system https://verification.fda.gov.ph

description.value: All categories of educational programs, courses, and attainment levels in the Philippines, based on the Philippine Standard Classification of Education (PSCED) published by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA)

All categories of educational programs, courses, and attainment levels in the Philippines, based on the Philippine Standard Classification of Education (PSCED) published by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA)

tr.td: Manages overall operations of an organization

description.value: The Occupation codes valueset includes Occupational Classification values from the Philippine Standard Occupational Classification (PSOC) published by the Philippine Statistic Authority (PSA).

p.style: border: black 1px dotted; background-color: #EEEEEE; padding: 8px; margin-bottom: 8px

div.p: Expansion performed internally based on codesystem PH Core Disability Type Code System v0.1.0 (CodeSystem)

tr.td: Complete or partial loss of sight/visual function.
Complete or partial loss of hearing/hearing function.
Complete or partial loss of speech or communication function.
Impairment in physical/orthopedic function including locomotor disabilities.
Significant limitations in intellectual functioning and adaptive behavior.
Neurological disorders affecting acquisition and use of listening, speaking, reading, writing, reasoning, or mathematical abilities.
Mental health conditions and psychosocial impairments.
Significant visual impairment not correctable by standard glasses/contact lenses.
Complete loss of vision or light perception.

title.value: PH Core Disability Type Value Set

p.style: border: black 1px dotted; background-color: #EEEEEE; padding: 8px; margin-bottom: 8px

tr.td: Complete or partial loss of sight/visual function.

Significant limitations in intellectual functioning and adaptive behavior.
Neurological disorders affecting acquisition and use of listening, speaking, reading, writing, reasoning, or mathematical abilities.
Mental health conditions and psychosocial impairments.
Significant visual impairment not correctable by standard glasses/contact lenses.
Complete loss of vision or light perception.
